topic: A coach has to cut a player from the team
raw_output:
[story 1]

The fluorescent lights of the locker room hummed with an irritating, high-pitched whine. Liam kept glancing at Coach Miller, whose face was set in an expression of grim finality. Every time Miller shifted his weight, Liam felt a strange tightening in his chest, like a vise slowly squeezing the air from his lungs. He fiddled obsessively with the drawstring of his sweatpants, the cheap fabric rough against his fingertips. "So, this is it, then?" Liam managed, his voice sounding thin and reedy even to his own ears. He didn't look at the team, instead focusing on a scuff mark on the polished floor, tracing its outline with his eyes. The silence between Miller’s pronouncement and the ensuing flurry of questions felt vast, heavy, and utterly suffocating. He could feel a prickling sensation along his arms, a nervous energy buzzing just beneath his skin, making his hands tremble slightly as he tried to gather his composure.

[story 2]

"We need to be realistic, Mark," Coach Davies said, his voice low and measured, which only amplified the sense of impending doom hanging in the air. Mark swallowed hard, the dryness in his throat making the action difficult. He kept running his gaze over the assembled players, cataloging their faces—some defiant, some resigned—but none of them seemed to offer any comfort. A cold slickness began to creep up his neck, despite the room being warm. He shifted his weight from one foot to the other, a restless, jerky motion that betrayed his internal struggle. *This isn't right,* a frantic voice whispered in his mind. He wanted to bolt, to find any escape route, but his feet felt rooted to the spot, heavy and unresponsive. He forced a small, brittle smile, the muscles in his face feeling stiff and unfamiliar.

[story 3]

I watched the clipboard tremble slightly in Coach Reynolds’ hand as he delivered the news. The sound of his voice, usually booming and confident, was strangely muted, flat, like a recording played too slowly. My stomach lurched. I found myself gripping the edge of the bench so tightly my knuckles turned white, the pressure a sharp, insistent pain. Every movement I made felt exaggerated, clumsy. I tried to focus on the pattern of the worn leather on the seat in front of me, but my vision kept flickering toward the doorway, anticipating something—a sudden confrontation, a harsh word. A clammy sweat broke out on my palms, and I quickly tried to wipe it away with the back of my hand, feeling a desperate need to hide the physical evidence of my distress.
